title: Configuring ODBC

Configuring ODBC

Configuring ODBC

Use the Uniface ODBC configuration file generator to generate the ODBC settings required for your assignment file.

To use the ODBC utility:

  1. Execute uodbccfg41.exe, located in the common/bin directory of your installation. The Uniface ODBC configuration file generator form is displayed.
  2. Connect to a new data source: After you have selected the database, the configuration utility detects data types available for that data source, detects connector settings for the data source, builds and displays a fragment of the .asnassignment file with the appropriate connector settings, and displays the data types supported by the connector.
    1. Click Select Data Source.

      The Microsoft Windows form Select Data Source is displayed. Use this form to add a new data source, or browse for an existing one.

      Choose Control Panel >Administrative Tools >Data Sources (ODBC) to display the Windows ODBC Data Sources Administrator form. For assistance with the use of these Windows forms, use the Windows help via F1 or by clicking the ? icon.

    2. On the Select Data Source form, click the Machine Data Source tab and select a Data Source Name from the list.
    3. On the Windows form Select Database, browse for the database to be used. To create a new data base, use the ODBC Data Sources Administrator form, mentioned above.
  3. Optionally, edit the displayed connector settings and click Save as to save the fragment to disk. You edit settings directly on the form, or you can edit them later. For information about the connector settings, see the options described in the Uniface ODBC Connector documentation.
  4. Optionally, edit the displayed data types and click Save as to save the data types to disk as an ODBC .cfg file. You can edit data types directly on the form, or you can edit them later. For information about the data types, refer to the Microsoft online documentation for ODBC Programmer's Reference. For information about how ODBC data types are mapped to Uniface data types, see the Uniface ODBC Connector documentation.

When the ODBC connector is loaded by Uniface, the connector reads the settings specified in the USYS$ODx_PARAMS setting and reads the data type file specified by the data types connector option. For example:

ODBC name=SQL_VARCHAR, SQL name="Char", Quoted=on, Precision=on, Scale=off, Max precision=254
ODBC name=SQL_LONGVARCHAR, SQL name="Memo", Quoted=on, Precision=off, Scale=off, Max precision=1073741824
ODBC name=SQL_LONGVARBINARY, SQL name="OleObject", Quoted=off, Precision=off, Scale=off, Max precision=1073741823, Prefix="0x"
ODBC name=SQL_DOUBLE, SQL name="Numeric", Quoted=off, Precision=off, Scale=off, Max precision=53
ODBC name=SQL_BIT, SQL name="Logical", Quoted=off, Precision=off, Scale=off, Max precision=1
ODBC name=SQL_TYPE_DATE, SQL name="Date", Quoted=off, Precision=off, Scale=off, Max precision=10, Prefix="#"

A line starting with a semicolon is considered to be a comment.

If the connector settings or the data type file contains errors, these will be reported as soon as an action (such as opening or closing a path) is performed on the connector.
